MOTION TO AMEND ORDER DETERMINING INHERITANCE RIGHTS, CALENDAR OF MAY-05-2021 - NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE MOTION TO AMEND ORDER DETERMINING INHERITANCE RIGHTS FILED BY PETITIONER W.C. COX & CO. IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE COURT WILL SET ASIDE THE ORDER DETERMINING INHERITANCE RIGHTS ISSUED ON OCTOBER 7, 2020. THE COURT DENIES THE REQUEST TO DISTRIBUTE TO THE CHILDREN OF THE POST-DECEASED HEIR AS REQUESTED. COUNSEL IS FREE TO FILE A NEW HEIRSHIP PETITION REQUESTING THIS RELIEF. (SEE TENTATIVE RULING FOR DETAILS) -

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Receipts Signedd By Uwe Dorrie And Gundela Holst Or Other Proof That The Funds Have Been Delivered To Them
Off Calendar Dec-07-2021 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Aug-26-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brianna Mannion appeared by zoom for San Francisco Public Administrator.

Ms. Mannion provided history, stated that Germany does not have personal representatives, that the last three pages in the notice of partial assignment nominate Mr. Cox to receive the German beneficiaries' distributions and that the checks will be made out to John Boessenecker in care of the beneficiaries. Ms. Mannion also stated that Mr. Boessenecker will cash the checks and put the money in his trust account. The court questions what happens once the money is in Mr. Boessenecker' s account and how does anyone have any assurance that things are going to go the way they are supposed to go. Ms. Mannion stated that Mr. Boessenecker is reliable, very experienced and that she trusts him. The Second and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is under submission with the court's intent to grant the petition.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Inheritance Rights
Off Calendar Aug-19-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Boessenecker appeared by zoom for W.C. Cox & Co. (Petitioner).

The Court takes judicial notice of all of the exhibits and papers that were previously filed in support of the vacated 11700 petition. The Court stated that it reached a certain level of comfort with sending this to Germany and letting them determine how this estate should be distributed. The Petition to Determine Inheritance Rights is under submission with the Court's intent to grant the petition.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Mar-02-2021 Continued To May-11-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Brianna Mannion via Zoom for San Francisco Public Administrator.
John Boessenecker via Zoom audio for Petitioner, W. C. Cox & Co.

The Court noted that it cannot override the order regarding distribution signed by a different judge, that is not persuaded by the evidence submitted regarding German procedures, that funds should be given to the personal representative or equivalent, that if they can't get a personal representative appointed they can open an estate here for Else Marie and there is an omitted heir that needed notice. Mr. Boessenecker stated the omitted heir was served statement of interest on Feb. 5th, that the shares are suppose to pass 70% and 30% to the heirs, that decedent died in Germany with property in San Francisco, that he will submit an amendment or declaration from Germany explaining how certificate of inheritance was obtained, and that he can file a motion to amend within the next week. Ms. Mannion stated she has no objections.

The second and final account and report of administrator and petition for settlement and approval thereof, etc. is continued to May 11, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Petitio For Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-31-2018 Continued To Jun-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet M. Boessenecker for San Francisco Public Administrator.

In response to an inquiry from the Court regarding the filing of an updated Status Report, Ms. Boessenecker stated that the Public Administrator had no plans to file a status report at this time. She said that when the Court entered an order appointing an heir hunter two months ago, she had requested that the next status report be set for twelve months later in order to give the heir hunter time to locate any heirs. She did not realize that another status report was due 60 days after that, but additional time was needed for the heir finder to do his job. She requested that the matter be continued for 6 months. The matter was continued to June 24, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Anasse. (Not reported)

Off Calendar May-14-2018 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-09-2018 Continued To May-14-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for Administrator

The Court noted that Mr. Weinkauf requested a 30 day continuance at the last hearing, he was given 60 days, and the petition is still not on file. Mr. Weinkauf explained that they are in the process of closing the estate, they think it is an escheat estate, they found documents in German, that he would like to follow up, and may pursue heir finder in Germany after the documents have been translated. The Court told Mr. Weinkauf this is the same thing he said at the last hearing and he should at least file a Petition for approval of report on status of administration as he is not ready to close the estate yet. The status on close of estate is continued to May 14, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le, Leslie Gomez. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-26-2018 Continued To Apr-09-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Continued To Apr-05-18 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf, for petitioner San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Weinkauf reported that the only asset of the estate was retirement funds in Germany. He said that so far the petitioner has been unable to locate any heirs, but that they have located some pre-war documents in German which when translated might disclose the identity of potentil heirs, but that the documents still require translation. He requested a 30-day continuance so he can prepare and file a petition for approval of status of administration. The matter was continued to April 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to appear was taken off calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-10-2018 Continued To Feb-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney to appear on February 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear, and why an inventory and appraisal, and a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have not been filed. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
